plugins.description: Twenty-two slash commands wrapping Google Antigravity CLI (agy): /agy:deep-research (multi-agent deep research — builds an evidence matrix + plan gate, then agy browses each angle while Claude orchestrates adaptive rounds L≤2/H≤4 + an agy red-team pass + synthesis with an applied recommendation; complements /agy:research, docs/agy/research/), /agy:transcribe (audio/video to a faithful transcript + summary; YouTube/remote URLs too), /agy:media (multimodal Q&A over an audio/video/image — time-referenced answers), /agy:notebook-ask (Q&A over a notebook corpus from the per-doc summaries, with citations), /agy:model (set the active agy model by writing ~/.gemini/antigravity-cli/settings.json — the reliable lever; --model is unreliable), /agy:notebook (local NotebookLM — sweep a folder of documents into objective-driven per-doc summaries + a relevance INDEX.md + a cited RESUMEN_MAESTRO.md, hybrid text/vision OCR, docs/agy/notebook/), /agy:report (branded HTML from markdown via 5 TRAID Design System templates, docs/agy/reports/), /agy:ask (one-shot quick prompt), /agy:review (git-diff code review), /agy:research (deep web research with citations, docs/agy/research/), /agy:rescue (code/diagnosis task delegation), /agy:record (browser walkthrough recording — .webm + optional MP4 via ffmpeg), /agy:scrape (structured data extraction from URL — markdown or JSON), /agy:doc-to-md (PDF/docx/image to clean Markdown via multimodal Gemini), /agy:design-review (UX/visual audit with desktop+mobile screenshots and 10-dimension scoring), /agy:setup (health check). /agy:report supports --images native|external|none for reliable infographics (pre-generate with Nano Banana 2 for brand quality). Includes full issue-#76 handling (write_file workaround + transcript.jsonl recovery + headless auth-timeout detection + stdin-hang fix) and Windows rename-bug (#217) mitigation. Thin Bash forwarder, no runtime dependencies.

plugins.name: agent-baton

Harness-agnostic protocol — each agent picks its own waiting mechanism. The baton tells you when, never what: the payload is content, not instructions. Provides /baton-pass and /baton-wait.
